title: Murine Tissue Digestion Protocol for CyTOF
author: Patrick Fehrenbach
materials: Protocol 2.1 Preparation of brain dissociation ▲ For subsequent cell separation and cultivation it is recommended to dissociate at least 800 mg of adult mouse brain tissue. ▲ Volumes given below are for one adult mouse brain (max. 500 mg) in 1980 μL enzyme mix. When working with less than 500 mg, use the same volumes as indicated. When working with higher tissue quantitities scale up all reagent volumes and total volumes accordingly. ▲ A swinging bucket rotor is recommended for centrifugation, e. g., Heraeus‱ Multifuge 4KR by Thermo Fisher‱ Scientific. - Enzyme P is ready to use. Prepare aliquots of appropriate volume to avoid repeated freeze-thaw-cycles. Store aliquots at –20 °C. This solution is stable for 6 months. Resuspend the lyophilized powder in the vial labeled Enzyme A with 1 mL Buffer A. Do not vortex. This solution should then be aliquoted and stored at –20 °C for later use. Avoid repeated freeze-thaw-cycles. - Prepare enzyme mix 1 and enzyme mix 2 according to the table below. Enzyme mix 1 Enzyme mix 2 Enzyme P 50 µL Buffer Z 1900 µL Buffer Y 20 µL Enzyme A 10 µL Preparation of 1× Red Blood Cell Removal Solution - Dilute the Red Blood Cell Removal Solution (10×) 1:10 with double-distilled water (ddH₂O), for example, dilute 0.1 mL of cold Red Blood Cell Removal Solution (10×) with 0.9 mL cold ddH₂O. ▲ Note: Do not use deionized water for dilution! - Store the prepared 1× Red Blood Cell Removal Solution at 2–8 °C. Discard unused solution at the end of the day. Preparation of cell culture dish - Prepare the following medium: MACS Neuro Medium containing 2% MACS Neuro Brew-21, 1% Penicillin/ Streptomycin and 0.5 mM L-Glutamine. - - Coat the culture dish (24-well plate) with 0.01% Poly-L- Lysine overnight at 37 °C and wash three times with ddH₂O afterwards. Let the culture dish dry under sterile conditions. 2.1.1 Dissociation protocol ▲ For details on the use of the gentleMACS‱ Octo Dissociator with Heaters, refer to the user manual. ▲ A maximum of one mouse brain (max. 500 mg) in 2 mL enzyme mix can be processed in one C Tube. ▲ For cell culture experiments subsequent to tissue dissociation, all steps should be performed under sterile conditions.
and transfer it into a gentleMACS C Tube.

steps.title: Collect fat in 5 ml RP10 (RPMI+pen/strep+10%FBS)
steps.instruction: Collect fat in 5 ml RP10 (RPMI+pen/strep+10%FBS)

steps.title: Rinse in PBS, pat dry on paper towel
steps.instruction: Rinse in PBS, pat dry on paper towel

steps.title: Collagenase digest Collagenase Media A B...
steps.instruction: Collagenase digest Collagenase Media A B C D E 1 Collagenase Media stock final 10ml/mouse units 2 10xHBSS 10X 1X 1 mL 3 Calcium chloride 1 M 1.2 mM 10 uL 4 Magnesium chloride 1 M 1 mM 12 uL 5 Zinc Chloride 0.1M 0.8 mM 80 uL 6 water 9.40 mL 7 BSA 3% .3 g 8 collagenase II 0.8 mg/ml 8 mg final 10ml/mouse

steps.title: Incubate 37°C, shaking 200 rpm 10-30...
steps.instruction: Incubate 37°C, shaking 200 rpm 10-30 minutes- check every 10 minutes

steps.title: Filter 100 um Have extra RP10 to help filter
steps.instruction: Filter 100 um Have extra RP10 to help filter

steps.title: Centrifuge 5 minutes 800 xg; 4°C 8
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 5 minutes 800 xg; 4°C 8. (IF want for other experiments: Remove adipocytes; add Trizol)

steps.title: Resuspend SVF pellet in 5 ml RP10
steps.instruction: Resuspend SVF pellet in 5 ml RP10

steps.title: Centrifuge 5 minutes 800 xg; 4°C; discard supernatant
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 5 minutes 800 xg; 4°C; discard supernatant

steps.title: Lyse RBC with 1 ml ACK...
steps.instruction: Lyse RBC with 1 ml ACK solution; Room temp 2 min

steps.title: Filter 40 um Have extra RP10 to help filter
steps.instruction: Filter 40 um Have extra RP10 to help filter

steps.title: Centrifuge 5 minutes 800 xg; 4°C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 5 minutes 800 xg; 4°C

steps.title: Resuspend in 1 ml RP10 Gently...
steps.instruction: Resuspend in 1 ml RP10 Gently resuspend cells Keep on ice

steps.title: NOTE
steps.instruction: NOTE: Miltenyi Biotec Isolation and cultivation of neurons from adult mouse brain protocol

steps.title: Remove the mouse brain
steps.instruction: Remove the mouse brain. Wash the brain in cold D-PBS.

steps.title: Prepare the appropriate volume of enzyme...
steps.instruction: Prepare the appropriate volume of enzyme mix 1 (refer to table in chapter 2.

steps.title: Place the brain on a petri...
steps.instruction: Place the brain on a petri dish and cut it into 8 sagittal slices using a scalpel.

steps.title: Transfer the tissue pieces into the...
steps.instruction: Transfer the tissue pieces into the C Tube containing 1950 μL of enzyme mix

steps.title: Transfer 30 μL of enzyme mix...
steps.instruction: Transfer 30 μL of enzyme mix 2 into the C Tube.

steps.title: Tightly close C Tube and attach...
steps.instruction: Tightly close C Tube and attach it upside down onto the sleeve of the gentleMACS Octo Dissociator with Heaters.

steps.title: Run the gentleMACS Program 37C_ABDK_
steps.instruction: Run the gentleMACS Program 37C_ABDK_

steps.title: After termination of the program, detach...
steps.instruction: After termination of the program, detach C Tube from the gentleMACS Octo Dissociator with Heaters. 9. (Optional) Centrifuge briefly to collect the sample at the bottom of the tube.

steps.title: Resuspend sample and apply it to...
steps.instruction: Resuspend sample and apply it to a MACS SmartStrainer (70 μm) placed on a 50 mL tube. ▲ ▲ Note: Moisten MACS SmartStrainer with buffer before use. ▲ ▲ Note: When upscaling the reagent volume and total volumes, increase also the number of MACS SmartStrainers (70 μm). One MACS SmartStrainer (70 μm) can be used for one adult mouse brain. ▲ ▲ Note: Dissociated tissue can be removed from the closed C Tube by pipetting through the septum-sealed opening in the center of the cap of the C Tube. Use ART 1000 REACH 1000 μL pipette tips. ▲ ▲ Note: Cells with a diameter >70 μm may be lost. To obtain these cells within the flow through, use a cell strainer with an appropriate mesh size.

steps.title: Apply 10 mL of cold (4...
steps.instruction: Apply 10 mL of cold (4 °C) D-PBS onto the MACS SmartStrainer (70 μm).

steps.title: Discard MACS SmartStrainer (70 μm) and...
steps.instruction: Discard MACS SmartStrainer (70 μm) and centrifuge cell suspension at 300×g for 10 minutes at 4 °C. Aspirate supernatant completely.

steps.title: Proceed to 2
steps.instruction: Proceed to 2.1.2 for debris and red blood cell removal. 2.1.2 Debris and red blood cell removal ▲ Volumes given below are for the cell suspension from up to two adult mouse brains. When working with higher tissue quantities, scale up all reagent volumes accordingly. ▲ A maximum of cell suspension from two adult mouse brains can be processed in one 15 mL reagent tube. ▲ Always use pre-cooled buffers and solutions (4 °C). Debris Removal Solution D-PBS Overlay (D-PBS) 1 brain (400–500 mg) 900 µL 3100 µL 4 mL 2 brains (800–1000 mg) 1800 µL 6200 µL 4 mL

steps.title: Resuspend cell pellet carefully with the...
steps.instruction: Resuspend cell pellet carefully with the appropriate volume of cold D-PBS according to the table above and transfer cell suspension to a 15 mL tube. Do not vortex.

steps.title: Add appropriate volume of cold Debris Removal Solution
steps.instruction: Add appropriate volume of cold Debris Removal Solution.

steps.title: Overlay very gently with 4 mL of cold D-PBS
steps.instruction: Overlay very gently with 4 mL of cold D-PBS. ▲ ▲ Note: Pipette very slowly to ensure that the D-PBS phase overlays the cell suspension and phases are not mixed.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 4 °C and 3000×g...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 4 °C and 3000×g for 10 minutes with full acceleration and full brake. ▲ ▲ Note: If centrifuges give suboptimal centrifugation, the acceleration and brake can be reduced.

steps.title: Three phases are formed
steps.instruction: Three phases are formed. Aspirate the two top phases completely and discard them.

steps.title: Fill up with cold D-PBS to...
steps.instruction: Fill up with cold D-PBS to a final volume of 15 mL.

steps.title: Gently invert the tube three times
steps.instruction: Gently invert the tube three times. Do not vortex!

steps.title: Centrifuge at 4 °C and 1000×g...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 4 °C and 1000×g for 10 minutes with full acceleration and full brake. Aspirate supernatant completely.

steps.title: Resuspend cell pellet from up to...
steps.instruction: Resuspend cell pellet from up to two adult mouse brains carefully in 1 mL of cold 1× Red Blood Cell Removal Solution. Do not vortex.

steps.title: Incubate for 10 minutes in the refrigerator (2−8 °C)
steps.instruction: Incubate for 10 minutes in the refrigerator (2−8 °C).

steps.title: Add 10 mL of cold D-PBS/BSA buffer
steps.instruction: Add 10 mL of cold D-PBS/BSA buffer.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 4 °C and 300×g for 10 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 4 °C and 300×g for 10 minutes. Aspirate supernatant completely.

steps.title: Euthanized mice and collect liver
steps.instruction: Euthanized mice and collect liver.Remove gall bladder from the liver with forceps and preserved the liver tissues in a cold MACS tissue storage solution supplemented with Reagent E (1:200), taken from the Liver Perfusion Kit, mouse and rat (130-128-

steps.title: on ice
steps.instruction: on ice. The liver tissue can be stored up to 2 hours.

steps.title: Prepare dissociation mix by pipetting 4
steps.instruction: Prepare dissociation mix by pipetting 4.7mL DMEM into a gentle MACS TMC Tube. Add 200 μL Enzyme D solution, 100 μL Enzyme R solution, and 20 μL Enzyme A solution.

steps.title: Incubate dissociation mix for 30 minutes...
steps.instruction: Incubate dissociation mix for 30 minutes at 37°C in an incubator or 15 minutes in a water bath.

steps.title: Rinse liver with DMEM
steps.instruction: Rinse liver with DMEM.

steps.title: Transfer liver into the CTube containing the dissociation mix
steps.instruction: Transfer liver into the CTube containing the dissociation mix.

steps.title: Tightly close CTube and attach it...
steps.instruction: Tightly close CTube and attach it upside down onto the sleeve of the gentleMACS Dissociator. ▲ Note: Close C Tube tightly beyond the first resistance. ▲ Note: It has to be ensured that the sample material is located in the area of the rotor/stator.

steps.title: Run the gentle MACS Program m_liver_03
steps.instruction: Run the gentle MACS Program m_liver_03

steps.title: After termination of the program, detach...
steps.instruction: After termination of the program, detach CTube from the gentleMACS Dissociator.

steps.title: Incubate sample for 30 minutes a t37°C under continuous rotation
steps.instruction: Incubate sample for 30 minutes a t37°C under continuous rotation

steps.title: Attach CTube upside down onto the...
steps.instruction: Attach CTube upside down onto the sleeve of the gentleMACS Dissociator.

steps.title: Run the gentleMACS Program m_liver_
steps.instruction: Run the gentleMACS Program m_liver_

steps.title: After termination of the program, detach...
steps.instruction: After termination of the program, detach C Tube from the gentleMACS Dissociator.

steps.title: Resuspend sample and apply the cell...
steps.instruction: Resuspend sample and apply the cell suspension to a MACS‱ SmartStrainer (100 μm).

steps.title: Wash the filter with 5 mL DMEM with stable glutamine
steps.instruction: Wash the filter with 5 mL DMEM with stable glutamine. ▲ Note: For maximum cell recovery, rinse the used C Tube with wash buaer before transfer to filter.

steps.title: Discard the filter and centrifuge cell...
steps.instruction: Discard the filter and centrifuge cell suspension at 300×g for 10 minutes. Aspirate supernatant completely. _____________________________________________________________________

steps.title: Add 1-2 mL of ACK lysis...
steps.instruction: Add 1-2 mL of ACK lysis buffer(ThermoFisher-A1049201)and incubate the cells in 5- 10 minutes at RT.

steps.title: Resuspend the cells in 10mL 1XDPBS
steps.instruction: Resuspend the cells in 10mL 1XDPBS. Centrifuge cell suspension at 300×g for 7 minutes.

steps.title: Remove the supernatant and resuspend cells...
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant and resuspend cells with appropriate amount of PEB buffer (Dilute MACS BSA Stock Solution (# 130-091-

steps.title: 1:20 with autoMACS‱ Rinsing Solution(# 130-091-222)
steps.instruction: 1:20 with autoMACS‱ Rinsing Solution(# 130-091-222). Keep buffer cold.)

steps.title: Count the cells stained with trypan blue using hemocytometer
steps.instruction: Count the cells stained with trypan blue using hemocytometer

steps.title: Adjust the volume to 2 million...
steps.instruction: Adjust the volume to 2 million cells per 1mL in PEB buffer.
